MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KAILYN J. SHARP, ESQ. of Delfino Green & Green (Tel: 415-442-4646) appearing for plaintiffs;
Attys. ALAN C. NOLLEY, ESQ. and TRACY A. TRIMBLE, ESQ. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-362-2580) appearing for defendant Delancey Street Foundation dba Delancey Street Moving (erroneously sued herein as Delancy Street Foundation)

The matter is argued by counsel for parties and submitted.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

RULING: Defendant Delancey Street Foundation's motion for summary judgement is denied. There is triable dispute whether Delancey Street moved and installed the murphy bed on May 20, 2015. Liberally construing the evidence presented by plaintiffs Kathy Austin-Robinson and Richard Robinson's and drawing all reasonable inferences from that evidence, the declaration of Valerie Stephenson-Boyd creates a triable issue precluding summary judgment in favor of Delancey Street. The objections to the declaration of Ms. Stephenson-Boyd are overruled. Moreover, the declarations of Ms. Austin-Robinson and the deposition testimony of Anthony Rivera also show that there is a triable dispute on the sole issue raised by this motion and the objections to that evidence are likewise overruled.

The Court signs an order in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (302/HK)
